content: TIER 3 — THE VOCABULARY OF CLEAR THOUGHT
Tiers 1 and 2 have given you the words to
express yourself and navigate the world.
Tier 3 gives you the words and concepts
that you need to express your opinions.
Logic. Cause and effect. Nuance. The
difference between correlation and
causation, or implicit and explicit.
Between plausible and proven.
These are the words that show up when
someone is reasoning carefully. Where
details matter, and everything hinges
on the specifics.
A NOTE FOR C1+ AND ESL LEARNERS:
English has a specific vocabulary for
argument and reasoning that many other
languages handle through grammar.
Words like 'albeit', 'notwithstanding',
'insofar as' — these carry logical weight
that conjunctions in other languages
don't always match.
Tier 3 teaches you how English signals
the processes of of careful thinking.
These words don't just describe ideas.
They structure them.
A hard fact about acquiring new language:
The dictionary definition is only one half
the battle when it comes to undersanding
what a word truly means.
The other half is connotation — the
emotional and cultural baggage the word
carries beyond its literal meaning.
'Slim' and 'bony' are both words for thin.
One is a compliment.
The other is not.
Same denotation. Opposite connotation.
Native speakers take on the connotation
of a word without thinking about it. For
people from outside of a culture, it has
to be learned word by word.
The example sentences in this pack are
designed to show connotation in action:
not just what the word means, but also
what it implies about the speaker, and
about the situation.
Pay attention to what's around the word.
Who's saying it. To whom. And in which
context.
The example sentence helps teach what
the definition is often lacking.
English has exact words for what makes
a person worth trusting — and exact
words for what makes them dangerous.
Integrity. Conscience. Rectitude.
These words name the good so precisely
that you can recognise it on sight.
Hypocrisy. Culpability. Complicit.
These name the bad so precisely that
no one can hide behind vagueness.
The cluster ahead gives you both.
The world gets clearer when you have
the right word for what you're seeing.
